skill.name: hmdb-database
skill.description: Access the Human Metabolome Database (HMDB) to search metabolites by name/structure/ID and extract chemical/biological/clinical fields when you need metabolomics research data or automated HMDB XML mining.
skill.summary: Search and parse the Human Metabolome Database to extract metabolite chemical, biological, and clinical data from XML dumps.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 28 static analysis findings are false positives after contextual evaluation. The HMDB scanner flagged markdown code formatting backticks as shell execution, XML namespace URIs as network calls, and the string 'HMDB' as a cryptographic algorithm reference. This skill is a legitimate metabolomics research tool that parses HMDB XML data locally using standard Python libraries. No actual shell commands, network requests, or cryptographic operations exist in the skill code.

low_findings.title: External commands pattern in markdown (false positive)
low_findings.description: Backtick-wrapped text in SKILL.md is markdown inline code and Python code block formatting, not shell backtick execution. The skill has no shell command execution. All 13 flagged locations are documentation formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged backticks are inside markdown code blocks or inline code spans in a .md file. No actual subprocess/shell calls exist in the Python code.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation (false positive)
low_findings.description: URLs in SKILL.md point to the official HMDB data source (hmdb.ca/downloads) and the skill source repository on GitHub. These are documentation references for data attribution, not network requests made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are in markdown link syntax inside documentation text. The parser script has no HTTP client imports (no requests, urllib, aiohttp).

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm reference (false positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'HMDB' as a cryptographic algorithm and 'inchi_key' as a hash. HMDB stands for Human Metabolome Database, a public scientific database. InChI keys are chemical identifier standards, not security-relevant cryptographic hashes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The pattern 'HMDB' appears in the context of database field names and descriptions. No actual cryptographic libraries (hashlib, cryptography) are imported anywhere in the codebase.

low_findings.title: Network reconnaissance in HMDB data fields (false positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged a taxonomy description in hmdb_data_fields.md as reconnaissance. The content describes biological taxonomy data fields (Kingdom, Super Class, etc.) available in the HMDB database - not any form of network or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a list of metabolite biological property fields. No system commands, network scanning, or data enumeration code exists.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance in SKILL.md (false positive)
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'Clinical data (disease associations, biofluid concentrations)' as system reconnaissance. This text describes types of clinical data available in the HMDB database for metabolomics research.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a feature description for a metabolomics database skill. It describes scientific data fields, not system information gathering.

content.user_title: Search and extract data from the Human Metabolome Database
content.value_statement: Metabolomics researchers often need to extract specific metabolite data from HMDB XML files manually. This skill automates parsing of HMDB XML dumps to search metabolites by name or ID and extract chemical, biological, and clinical fields programmatically.
actual_capabilities: Search HMDB XML dumps for metabolites by common name with case-insensitive text matching
Search HMDB XML dumps for metabolites by HMDB accession ID (e.g., HMDB0000001)
Extract chemical properties including formula, molecular weight, and description from each metabolite entry
Iterate through large HMDB XML files using memory-efficient streaming XML parsing
Provide a structured Python API (HMDBParser class) for integration into metabolomics analysis pipelines
Support cross-reference mapping through extracted HMDB accession identifiers
limitations: Requires a pre-downloaded HMDB XML dump from hmdb.ca; does not download data automatically
Only parses local XML files; no direct API access to the HMDB web interface
Search is limited to text-based matching on name and accession ID; no structure-based similarity search
Full HMDB XML dump is large (several GB) and requires significant memory during processing

use_cases.title: Batch metabolite lookup for research
use_cases.description: A metabolomics researcher has a list of metabolite names from an experiment and needs to retrieve chemical formulas and molecular weights for all of them at once. The skill parses the HMDB XML and extracts the matching entries programmatically.

use_cases.title: Cross-database identifier mapping
use_cases.description: A bioinformatics analyst needs to build a mapping table between HMDB accession IDs and external database identifiers (KEGG, PubChem, ChEBI) for data integration across multiple omics datasets.

use_cases.title: Clinical metabolomics data extraction
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher studying disease biomarkers needs to extract disease associations and biofluid concentration data from HMDB entries for a set of candidate metabolites identified in a study.

prompt_templates.title: Basic metabolite lookup by name
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to quickly find a specific metabolite's data in HMDB
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the HMDB database for the metabolite 'Caffeine' and return its chemical formula, molecular weight, and description.

prompt_templates.title: Batch search from a list of metabolite IDs
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a list of HMDB IDs from a research study and needs bulk data extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: For each HMDB ID in this list: HMDB0000001, HMDB0000002, HMDB0000003, search the HMDB XML and extract the name, chemical formula, and average molecular weight.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-reference identifier extraction
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to map metabolites across different databases for data integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Search HMDB for the metabolite 'Glucose' and list all its external database cross-references including KEGG, PubChem, and ChEBI identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Clinical data extraction for biomarker analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs disease association data for metabolomics biomarker validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract all clinical data from HMDB entries matching the metabolite list, focusing on disease associations and abnormal biofluid concentrations that may indicate biomarker potential.

output_examples.input: Search HMDB for metabolite 'Caffeine' and show its chemical properties
output: Description: A central nervous system stimulant found in coffee, tea, and various plants. Belongs to the class of xanthines.

output_examples.input: Find HMDB entry for ID HMDB0000001 and show its external identifiers

best_practices: Download the latest complete HMDB XML dump from hmdb.ca/downloads before running bulk extraction to ensure you have the most current data.
Use the memory-efficient streaming parser for large XML files by processing entries incrementally rather than loading the entire file.
Verify search results by cross-referencing returned data with the official HMDB web portal for critical research findings.
anti_patterns: Avoid relying on the skill to download HMDB data automatically; users must obtain the XML dump from the official HMDB website independently.
Do not use the parser for real-time or interactive queries on large XML files; pre-filter data or use indexed search for performant lookups.
Avoid modifying the XML dump format before parsing; the parser expects the standard HMDB XML schema with the http://www.hmdb.ca namespace.

faq.question: What is HMDB and what kind of data does it contain?
faq.answer: The Human Metabolome Database (HMDB) is a public scientific database containing detailed information about small molecule metabolites found in the human body, including chemical, biological, and clinical data.

faq.question: Do I need internet access to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill works offline once you download the HMDB XML dump. You need internet only to download the XML file from hmdb.ca the first time.

faq.question: How do I download the HMDB XML data?
faq.answer: Visit hmdb.ca/downloads and download the complete metabolite XML dump. Save it to a local directory and provide the file path to the HMDBParser.

faq.question: Can I search by chemical structure or SMILES?
faq.answer: Currently the parser supports text-based search by metabolite name and HMDB accession ID. Structure-based search is not implemented in this version.

faq.question: What Python version do I need?
faq.answer: The parser requires Python 3.9 or higher. It uses only the standard library xml.etree.ElementTree and os modules with no external dependencies.

faq.question: How long does parsing the full HMDB XML take?
faq.answer: The full HMDB XML dump is several gigabytes. Parsing time depends on your system. The streaming iterator approach keeps memory usage low but may take several minutes to traverse the entire file.
